channel.title: Man and Earth
channel.description: Recent content on Man and Earth

item.description: The Nice Warm Rock The road is an ecosystem. I know it is a wild idea, but it is true. The warmth of the surface attracting some animals, followed by the animals attracted to the road kill that get killed in their turn. Roads are criss-crossing every habitat, and the animals are like children without guidance when it comes to roads. This I find stupid, and yet on the road, society as whole becomes accomplice to the idea of progress.

item.title: License
item.description: license Creative commons This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 2.5 Generic Licens You may copy and share the work non commercially, without any propfit, as long as you, kindly refer to the original. Attribution — You must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made. You may do so in any reasonable manner, but not in any way that suggests the licensor endorses you or your use.
The Responsible Cat Owner The paradox of loving nature and owning a cat.
Journey, unplanned Those bugs that travel in our cars, I often think about these poor misplaced, perhaps anaware creatures…

item.title: About
item.description: ‘Man and Earth’ was an empty shell for almost a decade, in the early 2000s it was a promising phrase, it’s author (me), couldn’t be happier discovering that the url was not on the hotlist, and grabbed it and paid good rent since to keep it up, although the content changed with his(mine) fleeting love of the earth and the ever changing contract that was formed between the ever changing planet/nature/environment and himself(myself).

Thinking about this for a long time now First post to start this blog. Had some ideas but I am not sure how it will all come together. Had some ideas for some comics: The bee in the car One bird saved, one squashed New garden plant becomes an invasive nightmare Petrol and energy spent to feed a rescued bird Be a consciencious buyer, but the transport Bird reintroduction program, local birds in a threat Reintroduced bird decides to leave once established Swift colony, demolished building Swallow nests, council’s employment program Honey bee threatening local bee population Hedgehog introduced and then disaster follows ( eating eggs ) Newt fence, moving newts to an area with other newts, the whole population suffers Thought to be extinct Insect in a pile of rubble in London Enviormently conciencious cat owner, and mass bird grave in the garden

item.title: Hugo shortcodes
item.description: Images Dave Herring Dave Herring Github Gist Youtube video Tweet Hugo 0.24 Released: Big archetype update + @Netlify _redirects etc. file supporthttps://t.co/X94FmYDEZJ #gohugo #golang @spf13 @bepsays — GoHugo.io (@GoHugoIO) June 21, 2017 Vimeo Instagram Callouts 💡 I guess this works
